title: What is Dependency Injection (DI) and how does it work in Spring?

answers.junior: Dependency Injection means giving an object its needed parts from outside, not creating them itself. Spring uses DI to manage objects and their relationships automatically.
answers.middle: Spring supports DI through constructor, setter, and field injection. The IoC container creates beans and injects dependencies using annotations like @Autowired. Constructor injection is preferred for clarity and immutability.
answers.senior: Spring’s DI enables modular design and testability. The container resolves dependencies at runtime, supports profiles and qualifiers, and allows mocks for testing. Constructor injection avoids circular dependencies and is best practice.
answers.tricky: Spring resolves dependencies using bean scanning and context configuration. Circular dependencies are handled via setter/field injection, not constructor. Advanced DI features include custom scopes, profiles, and runtime bean resolution. Improper scope or circular design can cause runtime errors.
